channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Antisemitism and Community Safety: Julian Leeser's Concerns for His Son's Future
item.description: Jewish Liberal MP Julian Leeser addressed the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, sharing his eight-year-old son's fear of attending a synagogue following the Bondi terror attack. Le...

item.title: Somali Pirates Hijack Tanzanian Tanker, Move It to Somalia's Puntland Region
item.description: Suspected Somali pirates have seized a Tanzanian-flagged chemical tanker named MT ASANA in the Gulf of Aden. The incident occurred on Friday as the vessel departed Mukalla, Yemen. Seven armed pirates ...

item.title: 7 Sustainable Brands Promoting Eco-Friendly Living and Wellness
item.description: The article highlights seven brands that are leading the charge in sustainable living, beauty, and wellness. These brands focus on eco-friendly practices, ethical sourcing, and holistic approaches to ...

item.title: Education Challenges and Progress in the Philippines After SONA 2025
item.description: The article discusses the Philippines' ongoing efforts to address education challenges one year after the 2025 SONA (State of the Nation Address). While the government has implemented reforms such as ...

item.title: King Charles Opens Commonwealth Games in Glasgow
item.description: King Charles III officially opened the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow on July 24, 2026, marking a scaled-back version of the multi-sport event due to ongoing global challenges. The ceremony, held at Ce...

item.title: Blanchard's last-second 57-yard field goal caps Fajardo's 510-yard performance as Edmonton defeats Saskatchewan
item.description: The Edmonton Elks strengthened their position atop the CFL West Division with a dramatic 36-34 victory over the Saskatchewan Roughriders at Mosaic Stadium in Regina. Quarterback Cody Fajardo delivered...

item.title: Sustainable Energy Transition in South Africa: Lessons from the Limpopo Solar Farm Project
item.description: The article explores the complexities of implementing renewable energy projects in rural South Africa, using a solar farm in Limpopo as a case study. While the project aims to promote green energy and...

item.title: Security Forces Conduct Operations in Balochistan, Neutralizing Terrorists and Recovering Weapons
item.description: Security forces in Balochistan's Mastung district conducted operations that resulted in the killing of four terrorists and the recovery of significant quantities of weapons and ammunition. The operati...

item.title: Saudi Prince Dies in Hotel Room After Alcohol and Substance Overdose
item.description: A Saudi prince, Abdullah bin Fahad, died in his London hotel room after consuming excessive alcohol and other substances, according to toxicology reports. The incident occurred in November 2026 when h...

item.title: Kirsty McNeill's Transition to Africa Minister and Challenges in British Foreign Policy
item.description: The article discusses Kirsty McNeill's role as the UK's new Africa minister, highlighting her background as an aid campaigner and the challenges she faces in redefining Britain's relationship with Afr...

item.title: Stepfather sentenced for causing severe injuries to four-year-old child
item.description: A four-year-old boy has been left with life-altering injuries after being assaulted by his stepfather, resulting in a fractured skull, brain bruising, broken ribs and 34 bruises. The man, in his early...

item.title: Nigerian Man Charged with $300k Romance Scam Involving Fraudulent Emails
item.description: Okeoghene Patrick Udugba, a 45-year-old Nigerian living in Texas, has been indicted by a federal grand jury for money laundering and wire fraud conspiracy. Prosecutors allege he orchestrated a romance...

item.title: From Strained Relations to Strategic Partnership: Tinubu and Trump's Diplomatic Reset
item.description: The article details the transformation of Nigeria's relationship with the US under President Tinubu and former President Trump. Initially marked by tensions over religious persecution allegations and ...

item.title: Hikers face charges after off-leash dog encounter with bear in Banff
item.description: A group of hikers in Banff National Park has been charged following a video that captured their encounter with a bear while walking with an off-leash dog. The incident, which has sparked discussions a...

item.title: Pakistan's diplomatic efforts to combat Afghanistan's terrorism through SCO cooperation
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar emphasized during the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Council of Foreign Ministers' meeting that durable peace and stability in the region ...

item.title: Toronto Blue Jays' Flaming Dragon Ride-On Sparks Toy Store Frenzy in Kingston
item.description: The Flaming Dragon Ride-On, a plush dragon mounted on a stick, has become an unofficial good-luck charm for Toronto Blue Jays fans after appearing in the team's dugout during a series in San Francisco...

item.title: Kiambu County Achieves Record Revenue Growth Through Automation and Development Projects
item.description: Kiambu County has recorded its highest-ever own-source revenue of KSh 6.5 billion for the 2025-2026 financial year, marking a more than twofold increase from the KSh 2.9 billion collected in 2022. Gov...

item.title: Jennie Debuts Summer-Themed Single 'Less Than a Lover'
item.description: Jennie, a member of the K-pop group Blackpink, has released her new solo single 'Less Than a Lover,' which serves as an ode to summer and explores the nuanced dynamics of a relationship that exists be...

item.title: Website blocks bot traffic with access guidelines
item.description: The article discusses a technical message from News Corp Australia regarding bot traffic management on their websites. It explains that automated crawlers are restricted to prevent excessive requests....

item.title: Modi Announces New Law on Exam Paper Leaks, Higher Education Secretary Replaced Amid Protests
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi announced a new law targeting exam paper leaks during a midnight video address, promising swift action through fast-track courts and stricter penalties. The government has...
